blurb:
Root Beer is 13 years old and has a serious zest for life that would put dogs half his age to shame. This 85-pound Lab-Weimaraner mix has the heart of an explorer and his own unique hobby: relocating his food bowl around the house. His foster family calls it his version of interior design.

His favorite things are car rides with the windows down, leisurely walks where he can take his time, and any kind of human attention you're willing to give. He knows how to make the most of every moment and wants to share them all with you. Whether he's trotting beside you on a trail or settling in for head scratches, he's all in.

He's good with kids and gets along well with other dogs, so if you already have a calm pack at home, he'd fit right in. He's housetrained, fully vetted, and ready to go. Plus there's a holiday adoption special running through December 31st with half-price adoption fees.

Submit an adoption application at angelsrescue.org and email inquiry@angelsrescue.org about Root Beer.

Angels Among Us Pet Rescue
PO Box 821, Alpharetta, GA 30009
(877) 404-5874 | inquiry@angelsrescue.org
scoring_notes:
• Energetic 13-year-old defies age expectations, creating feel-good story angle
• Food bowl relocating quirk provides memorable and shareable newsletter content
• Holiday discount incentive adds adoption urgency through year-end deadline

blurb:
Snowberry has figured out door handles. Not just the round ones that most cats ignore, but the lever kind that actually open doors. She's seven years old and treats this skill like it's no big deal, which somehow makes it even better. She's a long-haired tabby who loves her crunchy food, lots of pets, and being brushed regularly. The brushing isn't a chore for her — she actually enjoys it.

She's extremely friendly and sociable, the kind of cat who's genuinely happy to see you when you come home. She'd be perfectly content as an only cat, but she doesn't mind dogs or other cats either. Basically, she's flexible about roommates as long as there are treats and attention involved.

If you've ever wanted a cat who can let herself into rooms you forgot to leave open, Snowberry's your girl. Just make sure you don't have any rooms you'd rather keep cat-free. Submit an adoption application through their website or email catinquiry@angelsrescue.org to get started.
scoring_notes:
• Highly adoptable with unique door-opening talent that's both charming and practical
• Compelling hook with her special skill makes for memorable newsletter content
• Flexible personality suits various living situations, broadening appeal

blurb:
Coconut Milk comes with a warning: she's got personality. The six-year-old has sass, keeps a sharp eye on everything around her, and operates entirely on her own terms. She's more reserved than outgoing, which means she's not going to be all over you from day one, but once she settles in, you'll have a cat with serious character.

She prefers calm company and cozy perches where she can observe life without being in the middle of it. The shelter describes her as having quirks, which in cat language usually means she's interesting to live with. She's half price through December 31st as part of their holiday adoption special.

If you're looking for a cat who's going to bring a little chaos and a lot of charm to your life, Coconut Milk might be perfect. She's not going to be boring. Submit an adoption application at their website or email catinquiry@angelsrescue.org to meet her.
scoring_notes:
• Holiday discount creates urgency and financial incentive for readers
• Personality-driven description appeals to readers wanting character over conformity
• Reserved nature may limit appeal but attracts specific adopter type

blurb:
Kathy is 14 years old and weighs a perfect 22 pounds. She's outgoing but wonderfully calm, with a friendly wag and bright eyes that still have that sparkle. If you're looking for easy, peaceful companionship, she's your girl.

She's crate-trained and handles alone time beautifully without any barking, chewing, or anxiety. She's making great progress on potty training and walks nicely on a leash for short, leisurely strolls around the neighborhood. She adores people of all ages and gets along well with small dogs too.

She's not one for constant cuddles, but her affectionate presence brings warmth without demanding attention. In her golden years, she'd love a single-level home with no stairs or just a few where she can wander comfortably and enjoy the simple pleasures of life by your side.

This resilient little lady has so much love left to give and would thrive in a calm home that appreciates a senior's gentle companionship. Submit an adoption application online at angelsrescue.org and ask about Kathy.

Angels Among Us Pet Rescue
PO Box 821, Alpharetta, GA 30009
(877) 404-5874 | inquiry@angelsrescue.org
scoring_notes:
• Perfect size and calm temperament ideal for seniors or small-space living
• Age 14 suggests long shelter wait, creating urgency for final loving home
• Low-maintenance senior appeals to readers wanting companionship without high energy demands

blurb:
Callaghan is fourteen and has figured out what matters: comfortable spots, good company, and checking out whatever you're eating. He's not quite a lap cat, but he'll plop down right beside you on the couch and settle in for whatever you're watching. He's curious about human food in that mellow, senior cat way where he'll investigate but won't knock your plate off the table.

He's diabetic and needs insulin shots twice a day, but he doesn't make it difficult. He doesn't mind being picked up, loud noises, or having his humans help with eye crusties. The shots are easy to give, especially if you add them to his mealtime routine. He's good with kids and other cats, but dogs aren't his thing.

If you want a loyal, cuddly companion who's already figured out how to be an excellent roommate, Callaghan's your guy. His diabetes shouldn't scare you off — it's manageable, and he's worth it. He's half price through December 31st. Email catinquiry@angelsrescue.org to get started.
scoring_notes:
• Senior cat with medical needs likely waiting longest, creating urgency
• Heartwarming story of loyal companionship despite diabetes challenges
• Holiday discount plus medical needs make this a compelling rescue narrative

blurb:
Doli showed up with no collar, no plan, but clearly knowing she was a good girl. She's 11 years old with sky blue eyes and thick golden husky hair that catches light like every shade of sunrise you can imagine. Her tail curls like a fox's and she does this little wiggle dance when her people come home that will make your whole day.

She's the perfect apartment dog because she rarely barks, but she still loves being outside for walks and car rides. Inside, she has what her foster calls the "bestest" manners. She sleeps in her crate or sometimes claims the master closet as her personal den. She won't chew anything up, though she admits she might dream about it.

Doli's shy when she first meets you but warms up quickly. She's not interested in making friends with other animals and would prefer to be your one and only. If you're looking for a calm, well-behaved companion who knows how to appreciate the good life, she's been waiting for you.

Submit an adoption application online at angelsrescue.org and ask about Doli. Email inquiry@angelsrescue.org to get started.

Angels Among Us Pet Rescue
PO Box 821, Alpharetta, GA 30009
(877) 404-5874 | inquiry@angelsrescue.org
scoring_notes:
• Stunning visual description and heartwarming arrival story creates emotional connection
• Senior dog with perfect apartment manners appeals to urban newsletter readers
• Long shelter stay implied by detailed backstory suggests extended waiting period
